compatibilityLabel: React DOM 19.2.6 root render/update/unmount e2e oracle

generation.method: checked runtime inventory plus exact npm tarballs and local Fast React React DOM placeholder copied into a temporary node_modules tree
generation.probeIsolation: one Node child process per target package, root-render-e2e scenario, and mode
generation.domEnvironment: controlled minimal DOM shim with deterministic mutation logs, sorted snapshots, and normalized React private marker summaries
generation.pathNormalization: temporary extraction paths, workspace paths, package paths, and React randomized private marker suffixes are not serialized

probeModes.reason: Default Node development probes root render/update/unmount behavior and warning surfaces with development diagnostics enabled.
Default Node production probes the same root render/update/unmount behavior without development-only warning output.

scenarios.description: createRoot returns a root object, marks/listens to the container, and does not mutate container children before render.
flushSync(() => root.render(host element with text)) commits a host tree synchronously and returns undefined.
A second root.render updates the same host child props and text through DOM mutations under the same root.
A later root.render replaces the root child type, removing old host output and placing new host output.
root.render(null) clears mounted children while keeping the root marker alive because the root is still mounted.
root.unmount returns undefined, clears root-owned children, nulls the public internal root slot, and clears the container marker.
A second root.unmount returns undefined and performs no additional root-owned child mutation.
root.render after root.unmount throws the React DOM unmounted-root error.
A single flushSync callback rendering two roots commits both roots before flushSync returns.
Development-only duplicate-root, render second-argument, and unmount callback warnings are captured without widening into unsupported behavior.

@fast-react/react-dom.source: local workspace package copied for placeholder behavior comparison

intentionalGaps.reason: The oracle executes published React DOM in Node with a deterministic DOM shim, not a browser engine or jsdom.
hydrateRoot, hydration mismatch behavior, and hydration replay belong to separate hydration oracle work.
Root listener installation may occur as a lifecycle side effect, but event plugin extraction, dispatch, bubbling, batching, and priority are separate oracle surfaces.
Controlled inputs/selects/textareas, form actions, resources, singletons, and Fizz/server behavior are outside this host-only root render path.
The scenarios use host elements and text only, with no hooks, effects, refs, class lifecycle, Suspense, portals, or context behavior claims.
The deterministic DOM shim does not model parser, layout, accessibility, focus, selection, CSS cascade, or custom element lifecycle behavior.

thrown.name: Error
thrown.message: Cannot update an unmounted root.

args.value: does not support the second callback argument. To execute a side effect after rendering, declare it in a component body with useEffect().
You passed a container to the second argument of root.render(...). You don't need to pass it again since you already passed it to create the root.
You passed a second argument to root.render(...) but it only accepts one argument.
does not support a callback argument. To execute a side effect after rendering, declare it in a component body with useEffect().
You are calling ReactDOMClient.createRoot() on a container that has already been passed to createRoot() before. Instead, call root.render() on the existing root instead if you want to update it.
does not support the second callback argument. To execute a side effect after rendering, declare it in a component body with useEffect().
You passed a container to the second argument of root.render(...). You don't need to pass it again since you already passed it to create the root.
You passed a second argument to root.render(...) but it only accepts one argument.
does not support a callback argument. To execute a side effect after rendering, declare it in a component body with useEffect().
You are calling ReactDOMClient.createRoot() on a container that has already been passed to createRoot() before. Instead, call root.render() on the existing root instead if you want to update it.

thrown.name: Error
thrown.message: Minified React error #409; visit https://react.dev/errors/409 for the full message or use the non-minified dev environment for full errors and additional helpful warnings.

thrown.name: FastReactDomUnimplementedError
thrown.message: [fast-react] react-dom/client.createRoot was called, but this placeholder has no React DOM behavior implementation yet. It exists to track the accepted react-dom@19.2.6 package surface; do not treat it as React DOM-compatible behavior.
